'奈良県奈良市佐紀町2701', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Saki Shrine (Nishihata, Nara)}} [[JA:佐紀神社 (奈良市佐紀町字西畑)]] {{wikidata link|Q56346769}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Saki Shrine |画像 = [[画像:Saki Shrine (Nara, Nishihata) Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}Saki-chō 2701 |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}・Six Mikata Deities |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = Ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, cypress-bark roof |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |文化財 = |ISO = JP-29 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 41|緯度秒 = 45.86 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 47|経度秒 = 27.71 |地図 = Nara city }} '''Saki Shrine''' (さきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nishihata, aza Saki-chō (さきちょう), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saki-chō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{sfn|奈良県史|p=28}}. === History === Its historical transition is unclear, but it is said to have been divided and transferred in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} from {{ill|lt=Saki Shrine (Saki-chō Kamehata, Nara)|Q56346760|WD=Q56346760|lt=Saki Shrine|1=Saki Shrine}} in Kamehata{{sfn|奈良市史|p=305}}. It is the tutelary shrine of Saki Nishi-chō (former Nishihata village), and the parishioner area is Nishihata, aza Saki-chō, west of the shrine. There is a miyaza system of upper six households and lower six households; with neighboring {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsuridono Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (former tutelary shrine of Mongaimura), it has nearly the same enshrined deities and miyaza system, and the branch-establishment period is also thought to be around the Edo period. Before that, the origin is said to be the subsidiary Ōkuninushi Shrine of Saki Shrine. Tradition says that repeated power struggles between Nijō village and Nishihata village led it to begin calling itself Saki Shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi}} - enshrined at the center of the honden{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} - enshrined on the right side of the honden{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. * Six Mikata Deities - enshrined on the left side of the honden{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. They are regarded as deities seated in the six mikata of Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōchi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. === Precinct === [[File:Saki Shrine (Nara, Nishihata) Haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden]] === Honden === An ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with cypress-bark roofing{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. === Haiden === {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} with tiled roof, measuring three ken by two ken, with boarded flooring{{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. === Water basin === It bears an inscription dated year 4 of {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125|1=Kaei}} ({{ill|lt=1851|Q7656|WD=Q7656|1=1851}}){{sfn|奈良市史|p=304}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === {{osm box|w|341688898}} {{commonscat|Saki Shrine (Nara, Nishihata)|Saki Shrine}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines|editor=Nara Prefectural History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meicho Publishing|1=UNKNOWN}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|ref={{sfnref|奈良県史}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=History of Nara City: Temples and Shrines Volume|editor=Nara City History Deliberation Council|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|year=1985|ref={{sfnref|奈良市史}}}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Saki Shrine (Q56346769)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Saki Shrine (Q56346769)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]]
